content: Molecular profiling essential for targeted therapy selection. Testing methods: next-generation sequencing (NGS) panels detect multiple mutations simultaneously; PCR for specific mutations; FISH for gene rearrangements (ALK, ROS1); IHC for protein expression (HER2, PD-L1). Tissue requirements: adequate tumor cellularity (>20%); FFPE tissue preferred; decalcified bone specimens often inadequate. Liquid biopsy (circulating tumor DNA) useful when tissue unavailable or for monitoring resistance. FDA-approved companion diagnostics required for many agents. Toxicity profiles differ from chemotherapy: EGFR inhibitors cause acneiform rash, diarrhea; VEGF inhibitors cause hypertension, proteinuria, bleeding risk; BRAF inhibitors cause photosensitivity, arthralgias, secondary skin cancers (squamous cell); HER2 inhibitors cause cardiotoxicity (monitor LVEF). Drug interactions common with TKIs (CYP3A4 metabolism); avoid strong inducers/inhibitors.
